title: Build UI: / (HUD) and /record pages
description: Build the React UI for the HUD (home page) displaying current mode, mic level, model status, and the /record page for long-form recording with transcript display and file rotation status. Use TanStack Router for navigation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement toggle mode with silence detection
description: Toggle mode with silence detection is still not wired into runtime behavior. Static review found the hotkey callback in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` always behaves like hold-to-talk, while `prefs.mode`, `prefs.silence_seconds`, and `prefs.silence_rms` are not used to auto-stop based on silence. `KeysHandle::set_enabled()` is also never applied from saved preferences, so left/right chord settings do not yet affect runtime behavior.
design: Implement toggle mode on top of the shared session/audio path used by hold-to-talk. The hotkey callback should branch on `prefs.mode`, start capture once on activation, and stop either on the next activation chord or when silence has exceeded the configured threshold. Use `AudioHandle::set_silence_threshold()`, poll silence duration from the active capture, and apply left/right chord preference changes through `KeysHandle::set_enabled()` so saved settings control runtime behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
1. When mode is `toggle`, the first activation chord starts capture and the next chord stops it.
2. Toggle mode can also stop automatically after `silence_seconds` of audio below the selected `silence_rms` threshold.
3. Hold mode continues to behave as press-and-release.
4. Left/right hotkey preferences actually enable or disable the corresponding chords at runtime.
5. The issue history records that this update came from post-review context, not a brand-new bug.

Tests for cpal audio capture initialization, device enumeration, resampling to 16kHz mono, RMS silence detection with configurable threshold, buffer management for long sessions, VAD-lite behavior for toggle mode auto-stop. Acceptance: All tests pass, coverage includes edge cases.

title: Fix macOS clipboard fallback to paste with Command+V
description: Clipboard fallback in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/type_.rs` uses `Control+V` instead of `Command+V`. On macOS this means the fallback path is likely broken in the apps where paste fallback is most important, including secure or finicky text fields.
design: Keep this fix narrow and testable. Update the paste chord to use the macOS command modifier, preserve clipboard restore behavior, and add focused tests around the chosen modifier so the fallback path does not regress again.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Clipboard fallback uses the macOS command modifier when pasting.
2. Clipboard contents are restored after the paste attempt.
3. Tests cover the modifier used by the fallback path.
4. Manual QA can confirm paste fallback works in a normal macOS text field.
close_reason: Fixed clipboard fallback to use Command+V on macOS. Added ModifierKey helper with regression tests covering the paste modifier, enigo key conversion, and bitmask values.

title: Harden permissions tests around macOS safety boundaries
description:
DESCRIPTION
The follow-up review on `stt-uwb` found that the new macOS permission tests in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/permissions.rs` are better than the previous broad enum-shape checks, but they still do not prove the most safety-critical behavior. Tests such as `test_microphone_request_opens_settings()` and `test_check_accessibility_uses_safe_api()` currently assert return values only, so a future refactor could reintroduce brittle shell commands, undeclared local dependencies, or other unsafe probing while the test suite still passes.

This matters because the project plan and `permissions-safety` workstream explicitly require side-effect-free permission handling and safe onboarding for microphone and accessibility access. The integration boundary lives in the macOS-specific permission code in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/permissions.rs`, especially the command-launching path for `request_microphone()` and the safe accessibility-check path that should remain based on `AXIsProcessTrusted()` rather than shell or synthetic-input fallbacks.
design:
DESIGN
Keep this issue focused on testability and contract enforcement for the existing `Permissions` API in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/permissions.rs`. Refactor the macOS-specific helpers behind small, testable boundaries if needed so tests can verify the exact onboarding command and safe-check implementation path without launching real system UI during unit tests.

Preserve the current product behavior from `stt-uwb`: microphone status intentionally degrades to `PermissionState::Undetermined`, microphone request uses settings-based onboarding, and accessibility checks stay side-effect free. If helper extraction is needed, keep the public permission commands exposed through `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` stable.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Permission tests verify the microphone onboarding path constructs the expected `open x-apple.systempreferences:...Privacy_Microphone` command or equivalent stable helper call, not just the returned enum.
2. Permission tests verify the accessibility check stays on the safe macOS API path and would fail if shell-based or synthetic-input probing were reintroduced.
3. Test names and assertions in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/permissions.rs` accurately describe what is being proven.
4. The strengthened tests run in CI/unit-test context without launching real Settings panes or requiring local machine-specific setup.

title: Replace HUD placeholder mic and recording state with live backend status
description: The home HUD in `apps/web/src/routes/index.tsx` still uses placeholder runtime state: `isRecording` is fixed to `false` and mic level is generated with `Math.random()`. This makes the status surface misleading and prevents the HUD from serving as a trustworthy quick-status screen.
design: Connect the HUD to real backend state instead of placeholder local state. Use the existing preferences/session data where appropriate and add a small runtime-status API if needed so recording state, current model, and mic activity come from actual backend status rather than client-side placeholders.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The HUD shows real recording status rather than a hard-coded `false`.
2. Mic level or activity reflects actual backend/runtime state instead of `Math.random()`.
3. The page continues to load cleanly when no active session exists.
4. The HUD becomes a reliable status surface for manual QA.

This test requires: stt-rtf (audio capture), stt-y3w (hotkey wiring), stt-pmb (transcription to typer), stt-1j5 (toggle mode), stt-bq4 (record capture), stt-rdp (file rotation), kb1 (permissions). All core implementation must be complete before manual QA can pass.

title: Implement local speech-to-text with whisper-rs
description:
Added local speech-to-text capability using whisper-rs with Metal GPU acceleration for Apple Silicon.

**Added:**
- stt.rs module: Model loading (3 profiles), transcription, voice commands, SHA256 verification
- lib.rs commands: load_model, transcribe, is_model_loaded
- Dependencies: whisper-rs (metal), sha2, hex

**Tauri Commands:**
- load_model(profile) - Load whisper model into memory
- transcribe(audio_data) - Run transcription on audio float array
- is_model_loaded() - Check if model is ready

**Model Options:**
- English Small (~75MB) - Fastest, English-only
- Multilingual Small (~75MB) - Fast, any language  
- Multilingual Medium (~250MB) - Slower, better accuracy

Uses Metal framework for GPU inference on M1/M2/M3 chips.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add regression tests for PTT session persistence contract
description: Static review of stt-t4k uncovered a remaining test gap around the real push-to-talk persistence contract. The backend runtime in apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs and apps/web/src-tauri/src/session.rs now creates sessions, persists typed or untyped entries, and ends sessions with computed totals, but the added tests mostly assert session totals and session mode rather than reading persisted Entry rows. That leaves the key integration boundary under-tested: whether the runtime actually writes the correct entry.source and typed values for hold vs toggle sessions and for typing-success vs typing-fallback paths. This matters because stt-1j5 and later HUD and QA work build on the same runtime path, and a regression here would silently corrupt session history without necessarily breaking existing total-count assertions. The relevant contract lives across apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs, apps/web/src-tauri/src/session.rs, apps/web/src-tauri/src/db.rs, and the Tauri command helpers start_session, end_session, add_typed_entry, and add_untyped_entry.
design: Keep this issue focused on regression coverage for the existing PTT persistence behavior rather than adding new product behavior. The tests should exercise the shared runtime and session boundary that owns session creation, entry persistence, and end-of-session totals. Prefer validating persisted database rows or a small extracted helper over relying only on indirect aggregate assertions. Preserve the current ownership split: lib.rs drives the hotkey runtime, SessionManager in session.rs owns active-session state, and db.rs defines the persisted contract for Session and Entry. Sequence this after stt-t4k; downstream runtime work like stt-1j5 should be able to rely on these tests as a guardrail rather than re-proving the same persistence semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Backend tests assert that a persisted PTT entry records the correct source for both hold and toggle sessions.
2. Backend tests assert that successful typing persists typed = true and typing fallback or error paths persist typed = false without dropping the transcript.
3. Tests read the persisted entry and session state directly, or validate an extracted runtime helper that proves the same contract, rather than relying only on aggregate totals.
4. The coverage is anchored to the current PTT runtime and session boundary in apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs, apps/web/src-tauri/src/session.rs, and apps/web/src-tauri/src/db.rs.

title: Unit tests: preferences load/save/validation
description: Per PLAN.md section 12: Tests: unit (preferences). Per PLAN.md section 'Preferences & Files', config stored at ~/Library/Application Support/sst/config.json. Tests config loading from JSON, saving, validation of hotkeys, silence thresholds, model profiles, typing options, voice commands enabled/map, record settings. Acceptance: All tests pass, validation rejects invalid configs.
close_reason: Completed - code already merged to develop

title: Verify and replace placeholder Whisper model SHA-256 constants
description: The hard-coded SHA-256 strings in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/stt.rs` look synthetic and repetitive rather than copied from upstream model artifacts. If those values are placeholders, model verification will reject valid files and make the model-management UI behave incorrectly even after the missing command surface is implemented.
design: Audit the expected hashes against the actual supported Whisper model files, replace any placeholders with verified values, and keep the filenames/hash pairs centralized so both model loading and model-status UI read from the same source of truth.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Every supported model profile has a verified filename and SHA-256 constant.
2. Valid model files pass verification.
3. Invalid or mismatched files fail verification with a clear message.
4. The model-status UI and backend loading path use the same source of truth.

title: Implement long-form record mode audio capture
description: Long-form record capture is not complete enough to treat as done. In `apps/web/src-tauri/src/audio.rs`, `RecordCapture::start()` spawns a capture loop using thread-local state instead of the `self` state later read by `get_and_clear_chunk()` and `stop()`. `run_record_capture_loop()` ignores `_chunk_duration_ms`, so backend chunking is not actually enforced, and `stop_record_mode()` discards the final `Vec<f32>` returned by `record.stop()`, risking loss of the trailing audio. Reopened because the original close reason claimed rolling transcript files, session management, and DB persistence were fully implemented.
design: Finish the record-capture backend before relying on UI or QA. Share live capture state between the CPAL callback and `RecordCapture` methods, enforce chunk boundaries based on `prefs.record.chunk_seconds`, flush the final partial chunk on stop, and keep transcript-file and DB persistence on the same path so no capture data is silently lost.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Record capture writes into the same buffer/state later consumed by `get_and_clear_chunk()` and `stop()`.
2. Backend chunking honors `prefs.record.chunk_seconds` without requiring the frontend to fake chunk timing.
3. Stopping record mode flushes any final buffered audio instead of dropping it.
4. Transcript file and DB entry creation stay in sync for every processed chunk.
5. The reopen rationale is documented in the issue history.

title: Write tests and perform QA
close_reason: Split into individual test tasks: stt-527, stt-atk, stt-51g, stt-ces, stt-8rv

title: Wire /record route to record-mode runtime commands
description: The `/record` route in `apps/web/src/routes/record.tsx` currently creates and updates database sessions directly instead of using the actual backend record-mode commands (`start_record_mode`, `stop_record_mode`, `transcribe_record_chunk`, `get_record_status`). As a result, the UI can claim recording has started while no long-form audio capture, chunk transcription, transcript-file writing, or rotation is happening. Runtime state is also inferred from stale open sessions instead of the live backend capture state.
design: Keep this issue focused on frontend/runtime integration. Replace the direct `api.sessions.create/update` flow with a record-mode API surface that wraps the existing Tauri commands. Use backend status instead of `ended_at == null` heuristics, poll or subscribe for live transcript chunks, and update the page so rotation count and transcript state come from real runtime events rather than placeholder local state.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Starting from `/record` invokes backend record-mode capture rather than creating DB rows directly.
2. Stopping from `/record` invokes backend stop and persists the final session state.
3. The page displays live transcript chunks and real record status from the backend.
4. Rotation count and current-session state reflect real runtime state.
5. Manual QA can exercise record mode from the UI without relying on fake session rows.

title: Capture the actual frontmost macOS app for session metadata
description: The current frontmost-app detection in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` and `apps/web/src-tauri/src/session.rs` uses `NSRunningApplication::currentApplication()`, which returns the STT app itself rather than the target app receiving typed text. That makes `sessions.app_name` misleading in logs and exports.
design: Implement frontmost-app detection using the appropriate macOS API for the active application, such as `NSWorkspace`, and keep the behavior best-effort so a detection failure does not block capture or typing. Make sure the value is captured from the target app context before typing begins.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Session metadata reflects the target frontmost app rather than the STT app itself.
2. App detection is best-effort and does not block capture or typing on failure.
3. The implementation uses the correct macOS API for active-application lookup.
4. Logs and exports show meaningful app names during manual QA.

title: Fix test compilation errors in prefs.rs
description:
The original issue claimed missing types in stt.rs, but actual failures were in prefs.rs:

1. Stale test fixture - test_preferences_custom_json_parsing had empty "map: {}" which failed deserialization because VoiceCommandMap had no serde defaults
2. Validation loophole - validate_preferences() had early return when both hotkeys enabled, skipping all other validation

Fixes applied:
- Added #[serde(default)] to VoiceCommandMap struct
- Removed early return in validate_preferences() that bypassed validation

All 140 Rust tests now pass.
design: Update this bug to identify the actual current Rust test failure before changing code. Start by running the Rust test suite and capturing the failing module(s). If the failure is in `prefs.rs`, fix the stale test fixture or serde defaults there first. If additional compile/runtime-wiring failures appear, split them into focused follow-up issues instead of keeping the bug scoped to missing types that already exist.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The issue description no longer claims `VoiceCommandMap` / `VoiceCommands` are missing from `stt.rs`.
2. The issue documents the current likely failure points in `prefs.rs` and runtime wiring.
3. Follow-up work from the updated diagnosis can be split into separate issues as needed.
4. The added context is visible directly in the issue fields/history.
close_reason: Fixed test compilation errors in prefs.rs: (1) Added serde(default) to VoiceCommandMap to fix stale test fixture with empty map: {}, (2) Removed early-return loophole in validate_preferences() that was skipping validation when both hotkeys enabled. All 140 Rust tests now pass.

title: Enforce and align preference validation across Rust and UI
description: Preference validation is inconsistent across the codebase. In `apps/web/src-tauri/src/prefs.rs`, `validate_preferences()` returns early whenever both hotkeys are enabled, which skips most validation for the default case, and `Prefs::update()` never calls validation before saving. The UI also allows values like `max_file_gb = 32` while Rust validation caps the value at 16, and one Rust test fixture deserializes an invalid empty `voice_commands.map` object.
design: Keep this issue focused on the schema contract. Enforce validation on save, remove the early-return loophole, align UI constraints with Rust bounds, and fix the Rust test fixtures or serde defaults so the test suite reflects the actual schema rather than an invalid one.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `Prefs::update()` validates before persisting settings.
2. Validation runs even when both hotkeys are enabled.
3. UI field constraints and tests match the Rust validation bounds.
4. The stale `voice_commands.map = {}` fixture is either corrected or supported intentionally.
5. Invalid settings are rejected consistently across backend and UI.
close_reason: Enforced and aligned preference validation across Rust and UI: (1) Added validate_preferences() call in Prefs::update() before persisting, (2) Removed early-return loophole in validate_preferences(), (3) Added #[serde(default)] to VoiceCommandMap to support empty map objects, (4) Aligned UI field limits with Rust validation: max_file_gb 32->16, throttle_ms 500->1000. All 140 Rust tests pass.

title: Build UI: /settings page - model management
description: Model management on `/settings` remains incomplete despite the UI being present. Static review found `apps/web/src/lib/api.ts` calls `get_model_statuses`, `get_current_model`, and `verify_model`, while `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` does not expose those commands. The page loader in `apps/web/src/routes/settings.tsx` depends on those calls, so the route is likely to fail during load and the Verify buttons cannot work. Reopened because the original acceptance claimed the backend command surface existed and was wired end-to-end.
design: Keep `stt-j53` focused on completing the model-management contract between the React settings route and the Tauri backend. Implement and register the missing commands in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, return structured model status from Rust, expose the current loaded model, and verify model files against real SHA-256 values from `apps/web/src-tauri/src/stt.rs`. Align the Rust command names and payloads with `apps/web/src/lib/api.ts` and ensure the settings loader can render without crashing when models are missing.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `/settings` loads successfully with `api.models.getStatuses()` and does not fail when models are missing.
2. `get_model_statuses`, `get_current_model`, and `verify_model` are implemented and registered in the Tauri backend.
3. The Verify action updates the displayed status for each model profile.
4. The command responses reflect the actual files and SHA-256 values in the shared models directory.
5. Review evidence is documented in the issue history so the reopen reason is clear.

Handle device enumeration, initialization, resampling to 16kHz mono format for Whisper, RMS silence detection with configurable threshold, buffer management for long sessions, and support VAD-lite behavior for toggle mode auto-stop.

title: Implement macOS permissions handling

title: Replace placeholder route tests with real route and API wiring coverage
description: Several route tests currently duplicate local helper logic or assert constants instead of rendering the real routes against the actual API contract. This means the suite misses important failures like the missing model-management commands used by `/settings`, and `apps/web/src/test/mocks.ts` does not even provide a `models` API despite the route depending on it.
design: Strengthen the frontend tests by rendering the actual routes/components, using realistic API mocks for every route dependency, and asserting behavior at the loader/action level instead of only checking copied helper functions. Prefer coverage that would fail if the route loader shape or Tauri API contract regresses.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Route tests render the real `/settings`, `/logs`, `/record`, and navigation surfaces instead of only copied helper logic.
2. Test mocks include the APIs each route actually calls, including `api.models`.
3. The suite would fail if `/settings` tried to call an unmocked or missing model-management command.
4. The improved tests cover route-level behavior rather than only formatting helpers.

title: Implement record mode file rotation
description: Record-mode rotation remains incomplete. `check_rotation_needed()` in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/audio.rs` uses `buffer_start_time`, but that timestamp is reset every time `get_and_clear_chunk()` runs, so `max_hours` will never be reached during normal operation. `update_file_size()` is still a stub, and `transcribe_record_chunk()` replaces the active `RecordCapture` during rotation without first guaranteeing that the old capture has been fully drained and persisted. Reopened because the previous close reason overstated the current `check_rotation_needed()` path as a complete no-data-loss rotation implementation.
design: Keep `stt-rdp` focused on safe rotation semantics after `stt-bq4` is corrected. Track elapsed session/file lifetime independently from chunk boundaries, compute or persist file size accurately, drain and persist all remaining audio before swapping capture objects, and keep session rollover explicit so `stt-83i` has a stable target to test.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `max_hours` rotation is based on session/file lifetime, not the most recent chunk timestamp.
2. File-size rotation uses a real byte count and not a stubbed method.
3. Rotation does not lose in-flight audio or transcript text.
4. Rotation creates a new session row only after the previous one is safely finalized.
5. The reopen rationale is captured in the issue history.

title: Implement audio capture module (audio.rs)
description: Implement audio.rs per PLAN.md section 'Audio Capture & Silence': cpal capture at device rate; resample to 16 kHz mono; RMS silence detection for toggle mode. This is a CRITICAL missing module - without it, PTT workflow cannot function.
design: Worktree: audio-pipeline | Docs: .agents/skills/worktrees/audio-capture.md
close_reason: Audio capture module implemented and wired through PTT workflow

title: Unit tests: model loading and SHA-256 verification
description: Per PLAN.md section 12: Tests: unit (model loading). Per PLAN.md section 'Whisper Integration', uses whisper-rs with Metal when supported else CPU. Tests whisper model loading from path, SHA-256 file verification against known checksums, profile selection (small.en, multilingual-small, multilingual-medium), Metal fallback to CPU, context reuse across sessions, missing/invalid model error handling. Acceptance: All tests pass, Metal fallback works.
close_reason: Added unit tests for model loading, SHA-256 verification, profile selection, and error handling

title: Wire session/entry persistence to PTT workflow
description: Session and entry persistence are only partially integrated. The backend has `SessionManager`, `start_session`, `end_session`, `add_typed_entry`, and `add_untyped_entry`, but the hotkey callback in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` still starts/stops audio, transcribes, and types text directly without creating sessions or entries. The callback also constructs `Typer::with_defaults()` instead of using saved typing preferences. Reopened because the original close reason claimed persistence was wired into the PTT workflow, but the primary runtime path still bypasses that workflow.
design: Wire persistence into the real hotkey-driven runtime path rather than only exposing commands. On activation, create a session using the current mode and source. On completion, persist the transcription as typed or untyped output, end the session, and compute session totals from actual entries. Keep the hold/toggle split explicit so `stt-1j5` can build on the same path instead of duplicating logic. Respect saved typing preferences when constructing the typer.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A normal hold-to-talk transcription creates a session row and at least one entry row.
2. Session `chars_count` and `words_count` are updated when capture finishes.
3. Typing behavior uses saved preferences instead of `Typer::with_defaults()`.
4. The hotkey callback no longer bypasses SessionManager APIs.
5. The reopen rationale is captured in the issue history.
close_reason: Fixed: session/entry persistence now uses active session mode

title: Build UI: /logs page
close_reason: Built /logs page with sessions list, entries display, search functionality, and export to text file

title: Implement Rust: typer.rs keystroke output
description: Implement typer.rs for keystroke output using rdev or enigo. Handle text typing, special keys (newline, tab), punctuation mapping, code block detection, mute in password fields heuristic, and ensure no stray modifiers in typed text.
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace destructive permission probes with safe macOS checks
description: Permission handling in `apps/web/src-tauri/src/permissions.rs` currently relies on brittle AppleScript shell hacks and a destructive accessibility probe. `check_accessibility()` sends `Cmd+X` to the frontmost app, which can mutate or remove user content, and microphone checks rely on brittle `osascript`/shell behavior that may produce false positives or false negatives.
design: Rework permissions handling around safe, side-effect-free checks and guided onboarding. Avoid synthesizing destructive keystrokes, centralize permission-state reads, and keep the onboarding/open-settings flow explicit when direct status checks are not trustworthy. Treat any fallback shelling as best-effort only after the safe path is exhausted.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Accessibility checks do not send destructive keystrokes to the frontmost app.
2. Microphone checks use a safer and more reliable mechanism than the current AppleScript shell hacks, or clearly degrade to `Undetermined` without side effects.
3. Request/open-settings flows remain available when direct checks are not possible.
4. Manual permission QA no longer risks mutating user content.
close_reason: Replaced destructive permission probes with safe macOS checks: accessibility now uses AXIsProcessTrusted() API, microphone uses explicit Undetermined fallback with settings-based onboarding

title: Scaffold STT project with Tauri, React, and TanStack Router
close_reason: Scaffolded with Tauri, TanStack Router, Biome, and beads tracking

title: Add regression test for invalid persisted prefs fallback
description: Prefs::new() now validates persisted preferences and falls back to defaults when the stored config is invalid, but the test suite does not currently exercise that startup-load path. This leaves a gap where a future refactor could regress persisted-pref validation behavior without failing tests.
design: Keep this focused on regression coverage for the prefs contract. Add a test around the persisted-config load path in apps/web/src-tauri/src/prefs.rs that simulates an invalid saved config and asserts startup falls back to Preferences::default(). Prefer a testable helper or temp-path-based approach rather than relying on the real user config directory. If practical, also add the corresponding positive-path test that confirms valid persisted prefs are preserved on load.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The Rust test suite includes a regression test that exercises loading invalid persisted preferences at startup.
2. The test verifies invalid persisted preferences do not remain in memory and defaults are used instead.
3. The test does not touch the real user config directory; it uses a temporary path or a testable helper.
4. If a helper is introduced for testability, coverage still goes through the same validation/fallback logic used by Prefs::new().
5. If practical, an additional test confirms valid persisted preferences still load unchanged.
close_reason: Added regression tests for persisted prefs validation: (1) test_load_invalid_persisted_prefs_falls_back_to_defaults verifies invalid config (both hotkeys disabled) fails validation, (2) test_load_valid_persisted_prefs_preserved verifies valid config loads correctly with values preserved.
